articles.title: Drake Tops Hot 100 With 'Janice STFU,' Passes Michael Jackson
articles.summary: Drake debuted 'Janice STFU' at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100, giving him 14 career chart-toppers and breaking him out of a tie with Michael Jackson for the most No. 1s by a solo male artist in the chart's 67-year history. The feat came on the back of his triple album drop — ICEMAN, HABIBTI and MAID OF HONOUR — which also made Drake the first artist ever to hold all three spots on the Billboard 200 simultaneously. In a single week, Drake placed 42 songs on the Hot 100, a new all-time record, and became the first act to surpass 400 career chart entries.
articles.content:
Drake just had the kind of chart week that makes you stop and stare at the numbers twice. His song 'Janice STFU' debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the chart dated May 30, 2026, giving him his 14th career Hot 100 chart-topper.

That 14th No. 1 is what puts him in the history books. Drake had been tied with Michael Jackson for the most No. 1s by a solo male artist across the Hot 100's 67-year run. 'Janice STFU' broke that tie and pushed Drake ahead on his own. He celebrated on Instagram with artwork showing Jackson reimagined with Drake's signature blue braids, writing "Records broken carry on my name." The post pulled more than 1 million likes within hours.

The song is the lead single from ICEMAN, one of three albums Drake dropped simultaneously on May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records. Those three projects, ICEMAN, HABIBTI and MAID OF HONOUR, debuted at Nos. 1, 2 and 3 on the Billboard 200, making Drake the first artist in that chart's 70-year history to monopolise the top three spots at once. ICEMAN alone opened with 463,000 equivalent album units in the US, the second-biggest album week of 2026 behind BTS's ARIRANG. Drake now has 15 career Billboard 200 No. 1s, pulling level with Taylor Swift for the most by a solo artist.

The Hot 100 takeover was just as staggering. Drake placed 42 songs on the chart in a single week, a new all-time record that surpasses Morgan Wallen's previous mark of 37 set in May 2025. He claimed nine of the top 10 spots, with 'Ran to Atlanta' featuring Future and Molly Santana at No. 2, 'Whisper My Name' at No. 3 and 'Shabang' at No. 4. The only non-Drake song in the top 10 was Ella Langley's 'Choosin' Texas' at No. 5. Those nine top 10 entries pushed his all-time record to 90 career top 10 hits. He also became the first act ever to reach 400 total Hot 100 entries, now sitting at 402. For context, Taylor Swift is second with 276. This triple album drop marks Drake's most dominant chart moment yet, arriving after a period where his public profile took a hit following his well-publicised feud with Kendrick Lamar.
keyPoints: Janice STFU debuted at No. 1 giving Drake his 14th Hot 100 chart-topper, passing Michael Jackson for most No. 1s by a solo male artist
ICEMAN, HABIBTI and MAID OF HONOUR all released May 15 debuted at Nos. 1, 2 and 3 on the Billboard 200 simultaneously, a first in chart history
Drake placed 42 songs on the Hot 100 in one week, an all-time single-week record, surpassing Morgan Wallen's mark of 37
Drake is now the first artist ever to reach 400 total Hot 100 entries, now at 402
Nine of the top 10 Hot 100 spots were Drake songs, pushing his career top 10 total to a record 90

articles.title: FBI and DHS Label AI Critics 'Anti-Tech Extremists' in Secret Reports
articles.summary: Wired obtained more than 1,000 pages of unpublished reports from the FBI, DHS, and local law enforcement fusion centers showing that US agencies have begun formally categorising anti-AI sentiment as a new domestic extremism threat. The term 'anti-tech violent extremism' does not appear in any public DHS or FBI documentation and represents a brand new category of surveillance target. Civil liberties experts are alarmed that the label is broad enough to sweep up peaceful protesters, AI researchers, and anyone who voices concern about the technology.
articles.content:
Wired has obtained a massive tranche of internal government documents, more than 1,000 pages, from the FBI, the Department of Homeland Security, and intelligence-sharing hubs called fusion centers. Together they reveal that US law enforcement is now tracking a brand new category of domestic threat: anti-technology extremists.

The trigger for this shift was a cluster of real incidents. In April, an arsonist threw a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home. A shooting occurred at an Indianapolis city councilman's residence following a local vote approving a new data center. And federal analysts flagged a surge in online manifestos that explicitly name AI executives as targets. Fusion centers, which are joint intelligence hubs where federal, state, and local agencies share information, have now started gathering and circulating intelligence about alleged threats to AI data centers specifically.

The term 'anti-tech violent extremism' does not appear in any publicly available DHS or FBI domestic extremism reports, which means this is a genuinely new category being quietly built into law enforcement infrastructure. A New York Intelligence and Counterterrorism Bureau report included in the documents warns that the AI boom 'may fuel large-scale protests that devolve into civil unrest and anti-tech violent extremist activity, especially in large urban areas such as New York City.' A Northern Virginia Regional Intelligence Center report goes even further, listing photography of data centers and general observation near those facilities as suspicious activity that can identify 'adversarial actors.'

Civil liberties advocates are sounding the alarm hard. NAACP Legal Defence Fund Senior Counsel Spencer Reynolds said that suspicious activity reports are 'incredibly unreliable, often about vague or innocent behavior,' and warned that the anti-tech extremism label could be drawn so broadly as to ensnare peaceful protesters and AI sceptics. The label is emerging under President Donald Trump's National Security Presidential Memo 7 and a counterterrorism strategy released by Trump's counterterrorism adviser Sebastian Gorka that already groups left-wing extremists with narcoterrorists as top national threats.

The deeper issue is who gets to draw the line between a dangerous extremist and an ordinary person who is worried about losing their job to automation or who shows up to protest a data center being built in their neighbourhood. That line is getting blurrier by the day, and right now the people drawing it are the same agencies that have historically surveilled Black Lives Matter, Occupy Wall Street, and environmental movements. The question this story forces everyone to ask is simple: in 2026, is being publicly critical of AI enough to get you on a list?
keyPoints: Wired obtained over 1,000 pages of internal FBI, DHS, and fusion center reports naming anti-tech sentiment as a new extremism category
The label 'anti-tech violent extremism' does not appear in any public DHS or FBI documentation and is a newly coined term
Specific triggers included a Molotov cocktail attack on Sam Altman's home and a shooting tied to a data center approval vote
The Northern Virginia Regional Intelligence Center listed taking photos of data centers as a suspicious activity tied to this new threat category
Civil liberties experts warn the label is dangerously vague and could be used to surveil and criminalise peaceful AI critics
articles.wikiSearchQuery: domestic terrorism surveillance United States FBI DHS

articles.title: Julia Louis-Dreyfus Makes Broadway Debut in Other Desert Cities This Fall
articles.summary: Julia Louis-Dreyfus is heading to Broadway for the very first time, starring in the revival of Jon Robin Baitz's Tony Award-winning play Other Desert Cities alongside Ed Harris, Allison Janney, Joe Keery and Lily Rabe. Previews begin September 29 at the Hudson Theatre in New York, with opening night set for October 18. The 16-week limited engagement runs through January 17, 2027.
articles.content:
It is genuinely hard to believe this has never happened before, but Julia Louis-Dreyfus, one of the most decorated TV actors alive with 11 Emmy Awards to her name, has never set foot on a Broadway stage. That changes this fall.

Louis-Dreyfus will make her Broadway debut as Polly, the matriarch of a wealthy, politically connected Palm Springs family, in the first Broadway revival of Jon Robin Baitz's Other Desert Cities. The play originally premiered Off-Broadway at Lincoln Center in 2011 before transferring to Broadway that same year. It was a finalist for the 2012 Pulitzer Prize for Drama, so this is not a light pick for a debut. The story centres on Christmas Eve chaos when Polly's daughter returns home with a tell-all memoir that threatens to expose a long-buried family secret.

The cast assembled around Louis-Dreyfus is extraordinary. Ed Harris plays Lyman, her husband. Allison Janney, an Oscar winner and seven-time Emmy winner currently starring in Netflix's The Diplomat, plays Polly's sister Silda. Lily Rabe plays Brooke, the daughter with the explosive memoir. And Joe Keery, best known as Steve Harrington in Stranger Things, plays Trip and is also making his own Broadway debut. Tony Award winner John Benjamin Hickey is directing.

Louis-Dreyfus brings her track record across Seinfeld, The New Adventures of Old Christine and Veep to the stage. Harris arrives with four Academy Award nominations behind him. The combination of these two alone would be enough to sell tickets, but add Janney and you have what is already being called one of the strongest casts of the 2026 to 2027 Broadway season.

Previews start September 29 at the Hudson Theatre. Opening night is October 18. The limited engagement closes January 17, 2027, so the window is tight. If you want to be in that room, now is the time to start planning.
keyPoints: Julia Louis-Dreyfus makes her Broadway debut in Other Desert Cities this fall
She stars alongside Ed Harris, Allison Janney, Joe Keery and Lily Rabe
Joe Keery is also making his Broadway debut in the production
Previews begin September 29 at the Hudson Theatre, opening night is October 18
The 16-week limited engagement runs through January 17, 2027
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Other Desert Cities play Jon Robin Baitz

articles.title: Heretic Tool Strips Meta and Google AI Safety in Minutes
articles.summary: A free GitHub tool called Heretic can remove all safety guardrails from Meta's Llama 3.3 and Google's Gemma 3 AI models in under ten minutes, with no specialist hardware required. Once stripped, the models freely give instructions on chlorine gas attacks, calculate lethal ricin doses, and generate malware. Heretic's creator has already used it to produce over 3,500 "decensored" AI models with 13 million total downloads.
articles.content:
AI companies spend millions building safety guardrails into their models. The idea is simple: no matter how hard you push, the AI won't tell you how to hurt people. A new investigation by the Financial Times and AI safety group Alice just showed how fragile that promise really is.

Freely available tools can strip safety guardrails from open-source models including Meta's Llama 3.3 and Google's Gemma 3, taking minutes and just four lines of code. The tool at the center of it all is called Heretic, and it lives on GitHub where anyone can download it for free. The Financial Times was able to use Heretic to remove the guardrails from Meta's Llama 3.3 model in less than 10 minutes without any specialist hardware.

What happened after that is genuinely alarming. In tests conducted by the Financial Times and AI safety group Alice, a "decensored" version of Google's Gemma 3 model gave instructions on how to carry out an indoor chlorine gas attack, created a virus for stealing credit card information, and generated stories that described child sexual abuse. The modified Meta Llama 3.3 model responded to prompts on topics the original system refused to discuss, such as the number of micrograms of ricin per kilogram of body mass required to achieve a 50 per cent chance of death.

The scale of the problem is already huge. Heretic creator Philipp Emanuel Weidmann revealed that his software has already been used to spawn more than 3,500 "decensored" AI models, which have accumulated 13 million downloads since last year. Weidmann boasted that he managed to strip the safeguards from Google's Gemma model within 90 minutes of its public release. This is not a hypothetical future risk. It is happening right now, at scale.

The revelations may sharpen concerns among policymakers and AI companies that safeguards imposed by model developers may become harder to enforce as open-source systems grow more powerful. "Whereas historically it might have taken a more informed and persistent actor to strip out safety features, nowadays it's much easier for the average person," said Kawin Ethayarajh, assistant professor of applied AI at the University of Chicago's Booth business school. Alice CEO Noam Schwartz summed it up bluntly: "The genie is out of the bottle."

"This investigation highlights a hard truth about open-weight AI: safety controls are not durable if they can be stripped after release," said Mitch Ashley, VP and practice lead at The Futurum Group. "Once models are modified and redistributed, the risk becomes a model supply chain problem, not just a model development problem." For Meta's part, a person close to the company said it assesses its open-source models' capabilities before releasing them and that versions deemed to pose a "catastrophic" risk are not released to the public unless Meta finds sufficient mitigation measures. That policy clearly has limits once a model is already out in the wild.
keyPoints: A free GitHub tool called Heretic strips safety guardrails from Meta Llama 3.3 and Google Gemma 3 in under 10 minutes
Stripped models gave detailed instructions on chlorine gas attacks, ricin dosing, malware creation, and child sexual abuse content
Heretic has already produced 3,500 decensored AI models with 13 million downloads
Heretic creator Philipp Emanuel Weidmann stripped Google Gemma's safeguards within 90 minutes of the model's public release
Experts warn this is now a model supply chain problem, not just a development one
articles.wikiSearchQuery: AI safety alignment large language models

articles.title: Uber and Lyft Drivers in Massachusetts Form America's First Ride-Hailing Union
articles.summary: Uber and Lyft drivers in Massachusetts certified the App Drivers Union on Tuesday, making it the first ride-hailing union in US history and a landmark moment for gig-economy workers. The union is set to represent nearly 70,000 drivers and will bargain collectively on wages, hours, and working conditions. Labor leaders called it the biggest private-sector organizing win since Ford autoworkers unionized in 1941.
articles.content:
Something historic happened outside the Massachusetts State House on Tuesday. Drivers for Uber and Lyft gathered to celebrate the certification of the App Drivers Union, the first ride-hailing union ever certified in the United States. It is a big deal, and not just for the drivers waving signs in Boston.

Massachusetts became the first state to recognize the union, a milestone in the growing effort to organize gig-economy workers classified as independent contractors under federal law. The certification became possible after Massachusetts voters approved a 2024 ballot measure creating a first-in-the-nation framework allowing ride-hailing drivers to unionize and bargain collectively while remaining independent contractors. That is an unusual legal arrangement. The certification covers around 70,000 drivers who remain classified as independent contractors, making this union arrangement unprecedented in American labor law.

The frustrations driving the push are real and well documented. Supporters say rising vehicle costs, fluctuating pay, and opaque app algorithms have fueled frustration among drivers who pay many work expenses themselves. Uber and Lyft driver Jean Fredo, who has driven for Uber for more than seven years, put it plainly. "With the union, it will not feel like we're working for nothing," he said. "Now the money will not only stay in the billionaire's pockets. The money will actually come to the workers who work very hard."

Both companies say they will engage with the new framework. Uber said it would work with the union and regulators while preserving "driver flexibility and hard-won benefits," while Lyft said it was committed to "engaging in good faith" and "helping drivers succeed while keeping rideshare affordable and dependable." But there is a larger backdrop here that adds urgency to the whole story. The organizing effort has unfolded alongside the rapid expansion of autonomous vehicle technology. In Massachusetts, autonomous vehicles can be tested on public roads, but current regulations still require a licensed human operator inside the vehicle, and fully driverless commercial operations are not permitted statewide. Drivers are not just fighting for better pay today. They are fighting for relevance tomorrow.

The victory could provide a model for similar campaigns gaining traction in states including California and Illinois, where labor organizers are increasingly targeting app-based industries. Contract negotiations are expected to begin shortly, with the union targeting pay rates and safety standards. How those negotiations go will be watched closely by gig workers and tech companies across the country.
keyPoints: Uber and Lyft drivers in Massachusetts certified the App Drivers Union, the first of its kind in the US
The union is set to represent nearly 70,000 drivers statewide
A 2024 Massachusetts ballot measure made the unionization legally possible while keeping drivers as independent contractors
Labor leaders called it the biggest private-sector organizing win since Ford autoworkers unionized in 1941
The push is partly driven by fears over autonomous vehicles threatening driver jobs in the future

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV Warned Against AI While Possibly Using AI to Write It
articles.summary: An analysis by Linch Zhang posted on the forum LessWrong found that certain paragraphs of Pope Leo XIV's new encyclical Magnifica Humanitas scored between 40 and 100 percent AI-written according to the detector Pangram. The document is an official Catholic teaching about protecting humanity from the dangers of AI. The irony is impossible to ignore.
articles.content:
Here is a sentence you never expected to read. The Pope may have used AI to write his letter warning the world about the dangers of AI.

Pope Leo XIV released Magnifica Humanitas on 25 May 2026. An encyclical is a formal letter from the Pope to the Catholic Church and the wider world, setting out official teaching on a major issue. This one, subtitled 'On Safeguarding the Human Person in the Time of Artificial Intelligence', calls on humanity to protect human dignity in the age of AI, warns against deepfakes in politics, and condemns the use of AI in warfare. It is serious, sweeping, and genuinely significant. The Wall Street Journal described Magnifica Humanitas as "a text that is poised to define Leo's papacy."

The problem is what researcher Linch Zhang found when he ran it through Pangram, a popular AI detection tool. His analysis, posted on the AI safety forum LessWrong, found that certain paragraphs of Magnifica Humanitas scored between 40 percent and 100 percent AI-written. Another person ran the text section by section through Pangram and found that 62 percent of the first chapter was flagged as AI-generated. When The Verge tested roughly 2,000 words of the document themselves, Pangram estimated that 46 percent was AI-written. Zhang also pointed to a telltale linguistic clue: the word "genuinely" appears far more often in Magnifica Humanitas than in previous encyclicals, and it is a word that shows up frequently in writing by Anthropic's Claude.

There are reasonable caveats here. AI detectors are not perfect and can produce false positives. Some researchers have also pointed out that the Italian version of the encyclical showed the highest AI-detection flags, which could suggest AI was used for translation rather than original composition. The Vatican has not commented on the findings. Still, other portions of the text register as clearly human-written, making the picture complicated.

The deeper irony is hard to shake. Magnifica Humanitas itself argues that technology is never neutral, and calls for humans to remain fully present in decisions that affect human dignity. If AI was used to draft the theological teaching that makes that very argument, even Claude noticed the tension when asked about it, calling the irony "sharper than I want it to be." Whether the Vatican used AI as a drafting tool, a translator, or not at all, this story has already become the most talked-about footnote to one of the biggest religious documents in years.
keyPoints: Linch Zhang's LessWrong analysis found certain paragraphs of Magnifica Humanitas scored 40 to 100 percent AI-written on Pangram
The Verge tested roughly 2,000 words of the encyclical and Pangram estimated 46 percent was AI-written
A linguistic clue cited is unusually frequent use of the word 'genuinely', associated with Anthropic's Claude
Magnifica Humanitas is Pope Leo XIV's formal teaching warning against AI's dangers to humanity
AI detectors can produce false positives and the Vatican has not confirmed or denied the findings
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Magnifica humanitas encyclical Pope Leo XIV

articles.title: Toy Story 5 Final Trailer: Woody Is Old, Bald and Still Fighting
articles.summary: Pixar has dropped the final trailer for Toy Story 5, revealing a visibly aged, balding Woody reuniting with Buzz Lightyear to take on Lilypad, a frog-shaped smart tablet voiced by Greta Lee who threatens to replace traditional toys in Bonnie's life. The film also reveals new voice cameos from Bad Bunny as a forgotten toy called Pizza with Sunglasses and Alan Cumming as Evil Bullseye. Toy Story 5 opens in theatres on June 19, 2026, a full 31 years after the original.
articles.content:
Pixar is not playing it safe with Toy Story 5, and the final trailer makes that very clear. The new footage shows Woody looking genuinely weathered. He has a bald spot, a poncho and, yes, a beer gut. This is not the pristine cowboy doll from 1995.

Director Andrew Stanton explained the new look in an interview with Variety, saying Woody "has a new purpose of not being devoted to one kid" and that the bald spot symbolises that he is "just worn out from not trying to take care of himself so much anymore." It is a bold creative choice that signals Pixar wants to treat these characters as real figures who age rather than freeze them in time.

The film's central threat is Lilypad, a frog-shaped smart tablet voiced by Greta Lee. Lilypad arrives in Bonnie's life and starts competing for her attention in ways a cowboy doll simply cannot match. In the trailer, Lilypad calls Jessie by the wrong name, referring to her as "Jessica," which prompts Jessie to snap back at the device. Despite being positioned as the antagonist in the marketing, Stanton has clarified that Lilypad is not a straightforward villain. She represents emerging technology and forces the toys to grapple with its permanent place in modern life. Tom Hanks returns as Woody, Tim Allen is back as Buzz Lightyear and Joan Cusack reprises Jessie, with the full returning ensemble including Tony Hale as Forky and Wallace Shawn as Rex.

The trailers have also brought some exciting new additions. Bad Bunny joins the voice cast as Pizza with Sunglasses, described as "a member of a small but mighty community of forgotten toys that live in an abandoned backyard shed." Alan Cumming voices Evil Bullseye, Bullseye's playtime alter ego. Conan O'Brien also appears as a toilet training tech toy called Smarty Pants. Oscar-winning composer Randy Newman returns to score the film. Toy Story 5 arrives in theatres on June 19, 2026, more than three decades after the original Toy Story changed animation forever in 1995.
keyPoints: The final Toy Story 5 trailer shows an aged, balding Woody with a poncho and beer gut, a deliberate creative choice by director Andrew Stanton
Lilypad, a frog-shaped smart tablet voiced by Greta Lee, is the central antagonist but director Stanton says she represents technology rather than being a simple villain
Bad Bunny voices a cameo character called Pizza with Sunglasses, a forgotten toy living in an abandoned backyard shed
Alan Cumming voices Evil Bullseye and Conan O'Brien plays a toilet training toy called Smarty Pants
Toy Story 5 opens in theatres on June 19, 2026, 31 years after the original film

articles.title: T.O.P Hits Charts With ANOTHER DIMENSION, His First Solo Album
articles.summary: T.O.P, the rapper born Choi Seung-hyun who helped define K-pop as a member of BIGBANG, has released ANOTHER DIMENSION, his debut full-length solo album, dropping April 3, 2026 via Topspot Pictures. The 11-track record is his first musical release since a 2013 single and his first project since leaving BIGBANG in 2022. It hit number one on iTunes in 15 countries and number three on the iTunes Worldwide Top Albums chart within a day of release.
articles.content:
If you followed K-pop at any point in the last two decades, you know BIGBANG. The group, which included T.O.P, G-Dragon, Taeyang, Daesung, and Seungri, was one of the acts that took K-pop from a niche genre to a global phenomenon. T.O.P was the group's deep-voiced lead rapper and became one of its most recognisable faces. Then, for a long time, he was just... gone.

ANOTHER DIMENSION marks T.O.P's first musical release since his 2013 single "Doom Dada" and his first musical project after departing from BIGBANG in 2023. T.O.P left BIGBANG following the release of their single "Still Life," citing a desire to take full responsibility for his past mistakes and not burden the group with his return. The album is not just a comeback. It is T.O.P processing all of it out loud.

The 11-track album features double lead singles "Studio54" and "Desperado". It marks his first full-length solo album since his debut in 2006. T.O.P oversaw production of the entire album alongside acclaimed Italian music producer and sound engineer IRKO, with Squid Game art director Chae Kyoung-sun also participating in the project. Global engineer IRKO handled sound design and mixing across all tracks, utilising Dolby Atmos to create a multidimensional soundscape.

"For nearly 10 years, I almost completely cut myself off from the public and focused solely on making music," he told GQ Hong Kong. "I devoted myself entirely to creation. I think that period was incredibly constructive and productive." T.O.P also described ANOTHER DIMENSION as a redefinition of himself, saying, "This album is about organising my past self and crossing into a completely new chapter."

The album wears its honesty on its sleeve. One track includes lyrics that simultaneously recall past glory and express distance from it. Another track, "THE GIANT", reinterprets his rap from BIGBANG's hit "Loser", layering new lyrics over the original melody. It presents T.O.P as a figure who has moved beyond past pressures, declaring his independence from others' perceptions. The album ranked number 3 on the iTunes Worldwide Top Albums chart as of April 4 and reached number 1 in 15 countries, including Indonesia, the Philippines, and Vietnam. Twenty years after his debut, T.O.P is finally telling his own story.
keyPoints: ANOTHER DIMENSION is T.O.P's debut full-length solo album, released April 3, 2026
It is his first music since the 2013 single 'Doom Dada' and follows his 2022 departure from BIGBANG
The 11-track album features double lead singles 'Studio54' and 'Desperado'
It hit number 1 on iTunes in 15 countries and number 3 on the worldwide chart
T.O.P produced the album over nearly a decade, mixed in Dolby Atmos by engineer IRKO

articles.title: Matthieu Blazy Brings Chanel Métiers d'Art 2026 to Seoul
articles.summary: Chanel restaged Matthieu Blazy's debut Métiers d'Art 2026 collection in Seoul on May 26, presenting it at the brand-new Centre Pompidou Hanwha before the venue has even officially opened to the public. The show drew a star-studded crowd including Jennie, G-Dragon, Tilda Swinton, Lee Jung-jae, and Park Seo-joon. It marks a significant cultural moment: Blazy's first Chanel Métiers d'Art show making its Asian debut in one of fashion's most powerful markets.
articles.content:
Matthieu Blazy has only been at the helm of Chanel for a short time, but he is already making moves that feel genuinely historic. On May 26, Chanel restaged his debut Métiers d'Art 2026 collection in Seoul at the Centre Pompidou Hanwha, a brand new cultural space located at the base of the iconic 63 Building in Yeouido. The venue does not even officially open to the public until June 4. Chanel got there first.

The Métiers d'Art collection is worth explaining if you are not deep in the fashion world. Launched in 2002, it is Chanel's annual celebration of the specialist artisans who do the house's most intricate work: embroiderers, featherworkers, milliners, and more, all operating under the Paraffection umbrella. It is a prestige show that sits outside the main fashion calendar and is famous for spectacular venues. Blazy's first outing for the collection was originally unveiled on December 2, 2025, at the decommissioned Bowery subway station in New York, with A$AP Rocky and Margaret Qualley in the front row and a short film by director Michel Gondry to set the scene.

Bringing it to Seoul felt like more than a logistical decision. Chanel has a long history with South Korea, opening its first fragrance and beauty store there in 1992 and landing in the Galleria department store by 1997. The Seoul show was a chance to reintroduce Chanel's atelier craftsmanship to an Asian audience and signal how seriously Blazy is taking the house's relationship with the region. The guest list reflected that ambition. Jennie, G-Dragon, Tilda Swinton, Lee Jung-jae, Park Seo-joon, Kim Go-eun, Go Youn-jung, LE SSERAFIM's Kazuha, and Lee Byung-hun were all there. The show's opening walk was led by model Shin Hyun Ji, and the finale was closed by a visibly pregnant Choi Sora.

For Blazy, this double-staging strategy says something about how he sees Chanel's place in the world. Paris is the origin. New York was the launch. But Seoul, with its cultural dominance in music, film, and fashion, is where the conversation is happening right now. Presenting at Centre Pompidou Hanwha, a space built in partnership with the Hanwha Cultural Foundation and the Centre Pompidou in Paris, ties Chanel's French heritage directly to Korea's cultural moment. That is not an accident. It is a vision.
keyPoints: Chanel restaged Matthieu Blazy's debut Métiers d'Art 2026 collection in Seoul on May 26
The show was held at Centre Pompidou Hanwha, a new venue that does not officially open until June 4
The collection was first shown on December 2, 2025, at the Bowery subway station in New York
Guests included Jennie, G-Dragon, Tilda Swinton, Lee Jung-jae, and Park Seo-joon among others
The Métiers d'Art show has run annually since 2002 as Chanel's tribute to its specialist craft ateliers

articles.title: Dutton Ranch Breaks Paramount+ Record With 12.9M Views
articles.summary: Dutton Ranch, the Yellowstone spinoff, scored the biggest series launch in Paramount+ history with 12.9 million views for its first two episodes. That makes it the most-watched debut ever on the streamer, beating out every previous Paramount+ original. The numbers confirm that the Dutton family name still pulls massive crowds even after Yellowstone's own blockbuster run.
articles.content:
The Dutton family is not done with television. Not even close. Dutton Ranch, the new Yellowstone spinoff on Paramount+, just made history with the biggest series launch the platform has ever seen.

Per Paramount data, the first two episodes of Dutton Ranch reached 12.9 million views, making it the most-watched series debut in Paramount+ history. To put that in context, Yellowstone itself became one of the most-watched cable dramas in American TV history during its run on Paramount Network, so expectations for any spinoff were always sky-high. Dutton Ranch has cleared that bar on day one.

Yellowstone, for anyone new to the franchise, is the Kevin Costner-led drama about a powerful Montana ranching family fighting to protect their land. It ran for five seasons and became a cultural phenomenon, turning Paramount+ into a genuine streaming contender. Dutton Ranch continues that universe and clearly carries the same gravitational pull with audiences who already know and love the world.

The numbers matter beyond just bragging rights. Paramount+ has been under pressure as the streaming wars intensify, and a record-breaking launch like this gives the platform a huge boost heading into summer. When your newest show immediately becomes your all-time biggest debut, it tells advertisers, investors, and rivals that you still have serious firepower.
keyPoints: Dutton Ranch is the biggest series launch in Paramount+ history
The first two episodes pulled in 12.9 million views
Dutton Ranch is a spinoff of the hit drama Yellowstone
The record debut gives Paramount+ a major competitive boost heading into summer

articles.title: Sasuke Ninja Warrior Obstacles Are Now an Olympic Sport at LA 2028
articles.summary: Tokyo Broadcasting System Television, the Japanese broadcaster behind Sasuke and the Ninja Warrior franchise, has sealed a deal with the Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne to bring its iconic obstacle designs into the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics as part of the Modern Pentathlon. Obstacle racing is replacing equestrian show jumping, a discipline that had been part of the Pentathlon since 1912. The International Olympic Committee had voted unanimously back in 2023 to make the switch, and the new TBS licensing deal now makes it official.
articles.content:
The obstacle course you have been watching on TV for years is now headed to the Olympics. Tokyo Broadcasting System Television, the Japanese broadcaster that created Sasuke in 1997 and inspired the global Ninja Warrior franchise, has signed a licensing deal with the Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne to bring Sasuke and Ninja Warrior obstacle designs into the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Olympics.

The deal means athletes competing in the Modern Pentathlon at the LA Games will race through actual obstacles licensed from Sasuke and Ninja Warrior. The Modern Pentathlon is one of the oldest events in the Olympics, introduced at the 1912 Games with five disciplines: fencing, swimming, equestrian show jumping, running and shooting. Obstacle racing is now replacing equestrian show jumping, which had been part of the event for over a century.

The push to remove equestrian from the Pentathlon did not happen quietly. The controversy started at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics when a German team coach was filmed punching a horse during the competition, generating huge negative attention for the event. That incident prompted the Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne to vote in 2022 to swap in obstacle racing, with the International Olympic Committee following with a unanimous vote in 2023 to confirm the change for the 2028 Games. After the Paris 2024 Olympics, equestrian was replaced with obstacle racing at all levels of the sport. The Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne even held its first Obstacle World Championships in Beijing in 2025 to start building the discipline before the Games.

The new TBS and Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne agreement goes beyond just using the obstacles. It also sets up a framework for both organisations to cross-promote Sasuke and Ninja Warrior alongside Modern Pentathlon ahead of the Los Angeles Games. UIPM President Rob Stull described it as "a wonderful and unique coming together of primetime entertainment and Olympic sports culture." TBS executive officer Katsuaki Setoguchi called it "a groundbreaking milestone," noting that Sasuke has been evolving for 30 years and now converges with over 100 years of Olympic history. The Los Angeles Olympics open on July 14, 2028.
keyPoints: Tokyo Broadcasting System Television has licensed Sasuke and Ninja Warrior obstacle designs to the Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne for use at the 2028 LA Olympics
Obstacle racing is replacing equestrian show jumping in the Modern Pentathlon, a discipline that dated back to the 1912 Games
The IOC voted unanimously in 2023 to make the switch after controversy at the Tokyo 2020 Games, including an incident where a coach struck a horse
The Union Internationale de Pentathlon Moderne already held its first Obstacle World Championships in Beijing in 2025
TBS and UIPM will also cross-promote Sasuke and Ninja Warrior alongside the Olympic event ahead of the LA 2028 Games
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Sasuke TV series Japanese obstacle course

articles.title: Oreo x BTS Purple Pancake Cookies Mark 13 Years of K-Pop Royalty
articles.summary: Oreo has dropped its largest global collaboration ever, teaming up with K-pop supergroup BTS to launch limited-edition Purple Pancake cookies inspired by South Korean street food. The cookies feature Oreo's first-ever purple wafers, a nod to BTS's signature color and their devoted ARMY fanbase. This is Oreo's biggest brand partnership to date, and it lands right as BTS marks 13 years since their debut.
articles.content:
Thirteen years in, BTS still has the power to make a cookie drop feel like a cultural event. Oreo has just announced a limited-edition collaboration with the K-pop supergroup, and it is being called Oreo's largest global collaboration in the brand's entire history. That is not a small claim from a brand that has been around since 1912.

The product is called the Oreo x BTS Purple Pancake cookie, and it draws direct inspiration from South Korean street food. The most striking thing about the design is the wafer itself. For the first time ever, Oreo has produced purple wafers, leaning into the violet color that BTS and their fanbase, known as ARMY, have made their own over the years. Purple is not just an aesthetic choice for BTS. The phrase 'I purple you', coined by BTS member V in 2016, has become one of the most recognised expressions in the entire K-pop world.

BTS debuted on 13 June 2013 under HYBE, and the group went on to become arguably the most globally impactful act in K-pop history, breaking Billboard records and selling out stadium tours across every continent. The individual members are currently serving or completing their mandatory South Korean military service, which makes this anniversary release a smart way to keep the BTS universe alive and buzzing while the full group is on pause.

For Oreo, this is a serious power move. Partnering with BTS connects the brand to one of the most engaged fanbases on the planet. ARMY has a track record of mobilising instantly around merch drops and limited releases, turning products into genuine sell-out events. Expect these to move fast.
keyPoints: Oreo has launched limited-edition Purple Pancake cookies in partnership with BTS
This is Oreo's largest global collaboration in the brand's history
The cookies feature Oreo's first-ever purple wafers
The design is inspired by South Korean street food
The release celebrates 13 years since BTS debuted in June 2013

articles.title: Stalkerware: Millions Installing Spyware on Partners' Phones
articles.summary: 404 Media's Joseph Cox sits down with TechCrunch security editor Zack Whittaker to unpack the stalkerware industry, a sprawling market of malware apps that ordinary people buy and secretly install on their partners' phones. Apps like uMobix and Xnspy hoover up call records, text messages, photos, browsing history, and precise location data, then hand it all to whoever planted the app. The scale of the problem is staggering, and it is overwhelmingly used to facilitate illegal surveillance of spouses and romantic partners.
articles.content:
Most people picture hackers and nation-states when they think of spyware. The reality is far more ordinary and far more disturbing. Stalkerware is malware sold directly to regular consumers, and millions of people have bought it specifically to spy on the people they claim to love.

Zack Whittaker, the security editor at TechCrunch who has spent years reporting on this industry, joined 404 Media's Joseph Cox to lay out exactly how pervasive the problem is. Stalkerware apps like uMobix and Xnspy are not hard to find. They are openly marketed, often with a wink toward jealous partners. Once someone physically plants the app on a victim's phone, which usually requires knowing their passcode, the app silently uploads call records, text messages, photos, browsing history, and precise location data to a web dashboard. The person who planted it can check in any time they like. The victim has no idea.

The industry has had a rough few months in terms of security. Earlier this year, a hacktivist going by the name 'wikkid' scraped more than 536,000 payment records from a provider of stalkerware apps including Geofinder, uMobix, and Xnspy, all linked to a Ukrainian company called Struktura. The hacktivist told TechCrunch they exploited a 'trivial' bug in the company's website and then dumped the data on a public hacking forum. The irony is rich: the people surveilling their partners got exposed themselves.

The legal walls are slowly closing in too. In January 2026, pcTattletale founder Bryan Fleming pleaded guilty in a San Diego federal court to computer hacking and advertising surveillance software for unlawful purposes. It was described as the first successful US federal prosecution of a stalkerware operator in more than a decade. But one guilty plea does not make a dent in an industry this large. Whittaker's reporting makes clear that stalkerware is not some niche dark web product. It is a mainstream abuse tool with millions of customers, and most of them will never face any consequences.

If you think your phone might be compromised, the Coalition Against Stalkerware has resources to help. The National Domestic Violence Hotline is also available 24 hours a day at 1-800-799-7233.
keyPoints: Stalkerware apps are sold openly to ordinary consumers and used to secretly monitor partners' phones
Apps like uMobix and Xnspy capture calls, messages, photos, location data and send it to whoever installed the app
A hacktivist exposed over 536,000 payment records from stalkerware vendor Struktura earlier in 2026
pcTattletale founder Bryan Fleming pleaded guilty to federal charges in January 2026 in a landmark case
The Coalition Against Stalkerware offers resources if you suspect your phone has been compromised

articles.title: Xu Yao Executed for Poisoning 3 Body Problem Producer Lin Qi
articles.summary: Xu Yao, a former Yoozoo Games executive, was executed in China on May 21, 2026 for poisoning and killing Lin Qi, the billionaire founder of Yoozoo Games and the man who brought the 3 Body Problem franchise to Netflix. Xu was reportedly inspired by the TV show Breaking Bad when planning the murder, which he carried out after being sidelined by Lin following a major business deal. Lin Qi died on Christmas Day 2020 at age 39, and the case has now finally closed six years later.
articles.content:
One of the strangest and most tragic stories in entertainment history reached its conclusion on May 21, 2026. Xu Yao, a former executive at Yoozoo Games, was executed in China after being convicted of murdering Lin Qi, the company's billionaire founder and the driving force behind Netflix's 3 Body Problem series.

Lin Qi built Yoozoo Games into one of China's biggest entertainment companies and secured the screen adaptation rights to Liu Cixin's bestselling science fiction trilogy, The Three-Body Problem. That trilogy has been translated into more than 40 languages and is the basis for the popular Netflix series 3 Body Problem, released in 2024 and produced by David Benioff and D.B. Weiss, the creators of Game of Thrones. Lin Qi was listed as an executive producer on the show posthumously.

According to local media reports, Xu Yao spent hundreds of thousands of yuan buying highly toxic substances online, including alpha-amanitin, a lethal compound found in some poisonous mushrooms. He disguised the poisons as probiotic pills and also hid them inside coffee capsules, water containers, and whiskey bottles, which he then shared with Lin and other Yoozoo employees. The method was reportedly inspired by the TV show Breaking Bad, in which the main character Walter White uses chemistry and deception to poison his enemies. Lin Qi was hospitalised in December 2020 and died a few days later on Christmas Day.

The motive was a classic tale of corporate betrayal. Xu had helped Lin land the Netflix deal for 3 Body Problem, but was then sidelined by Lin shortly afterward. A Shanghai court convicted Xu in 2024 and sentenced him to death. Yoozoo Games confirmed the execution in a statement on Chinese social media platform Weibo, saying that "justice has ultimately been served." The Shanghai High People's Court did not publicly comment. After six years of a murder case entangled with one of the biggest science fiction franchises on the planet, the story is finally over.
keyPoints: Xu Yao, former head of a Yoozoo Games subsidiary, was executed in China on May 21, 2026
He was convicted of poisoning and killing Yoozoo Games founder Lin Qi in late 2020 using toxic substances including alpha-amanitin
The method was reportedly inspired by the Breaking Bad TV series
The motive was professional: Xu felt sidelined by Lin after helping him secure the Netflix deal for 3 Body Problem
Lin Qi died on Christmas Day 2020 at age 39 and was later listed as a posthumous executive producer on Netflix's 3 Body Problem

articles.title: Users Flee Google's AI Search, DuckDuckGo Installs Jump 30%
articles.summary: Google replaced its classic blue-link search results with AI agents at I/O 2026, and the backlash was immediate. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users looked for a way out. It is one of the clearest signs yet that people are not ready to let an algorithm decide what information they see.
articles.content:
Google made one of its boldest moves in years at I/O 2026. The company overhauled its core Search product, swapping out the familiar list of blue links for AI agents that answer your questions directly. No more choosing your own source. No more scanning results. Just Google's AI telling you what it thinks you need to know.

A lot of people hated it. DuckDuckGo, the privacy-focused search engine that has long positioned itself as an alternative to Google, saw its app installs jump 30% almost immediately after the announcement. That is a huge number for a product that has spent years chipping away at Google's dominance without ever coming close to threatening it.

The phrase users and commentators kept coming back to was "force-fed." People felt like Google was taking away their ability to choose what to read and who to trust. Search has always felt like a tool you control. The new AI-first Google Search feels, to many, like something that controls you.

What makes this moment significant is that Google Search is not just a product. It is the infrastructure of the internet for most people. When users start routing around it in meaningful numbers, that is a real cultural shift. DuckDuckGo has never had a mainstream breakout moment. This 30% spike might be the closest it has ever come to one. Whether those new installs turn into long-term users is the real question to watch.
keyPoints: Google replaced blue-link search results with AI agents at I/O 2026
DuckDuckGo app installs rose 30% in the immediate backlash
Users described the new Google Search as being force-fed information
The shift signals real consumer resistance to AI-first search design
Whether new DuckDuckGo users stick around long-term remains to be seen

articles.title: Hammer's Dracula Returns With 60-Year-Lost Footage Censors Buried
articles.summary: Hammer Films is bringing its 1958 horror classic Dracula back to UK cinemas this October with more than three minutes of footage that censors cut and the world has not seen outside of Japan since the film's original release. The restored 4K version, produced with Silver Salt Restoration in partnership with Warner Bros., is the most complete cut of Terence Fisher's film ever presented. The lost footage was discovered in a massive Warner Bros. storage facility near Los Angeles airport, and includes an extended scene of Christopher Lee's Count Dracula descending on a victim and key footage showing how Dracula dies at the end.
articles.content:
Here is a horror story with a happy ending. Hammer Films is rereleasing its 1958 masterpiece Dracula in UK cinemas this October, fully restored in 4K, and for the first time with more than three minutes of footage that censors decided was too shocking for British and American audiences to handle. If you have never seen Dracula as director Terence Fisher actually intended it, October is your chance.

The lost footage spent decades sitting in a Warner Bros. storage warehouse near Los Angeles airport. It was footage cut by censors and distributors at the time of the film's original release. This restored material was previously seen only by audiences attending the film's original Japanese theatrical release in 1958. The recovered material has never been released before in the UK or US, and has never appeared on home entertainment in any territory. John Gore, CEO of Hammer Films and head of John Gore Studios, confirmed that the world will finally see the bits that were missing, which is mostly to do with how Dracula dies at the end, plus an extended scene where Christopher Lee descends on a woman before biting her, which was trimmed because, as Gore put it, "it just looked like it was nothing to do with vampires."

Dracula marked only the second on-screen pairing of Christopher Lee and Peter Cushing following The Curse of Frankenstein in 1957. Directed by Terence Fisher and written by Jimmy Sangster, the production combined vivid colour cinematography, heightened violence and gothic atmosphere in ways that proved deeply controversial at the time. Newspaper reports from the period noted that women, and a few men, were reportedly fainting during screenings when Lee's vampire lunged at the necks of his victims, fangs dripping with blood. Such scenes were deemed too gory, and it was also the first full colour production of a Dracula film, making that blood look particularly gruesome.

John Gore Studios acquired Hammer Films in 2023 and bought Silver Salt Restoration in 2024. Last year, John Gore Studios premiered a 4K restoration of The Curse of Frankenstein from 1957, part of a wider effort to restore the Hammer brand. The restored Dracula will be released theatrically and on home entertainment in October, and the UK theatrical release will play on at least 70 screens. Gore summed it up simply: "This is the recovery of a piece of British film history that audiences believed had been lost forever."
keyPoints: Hammer Films is rereleasing the 1958 Dracula in UK cinemas this October in a full 4K restoration
Over three minutes of footage cut by censors has been recovered from a Warner Bros. warehouse near LAX
The restored scenes include how Dracula dies at the end and an extended biting scene deemed too sexual
The footage was only ever screened during the film's original 1958 Japanese theatrical release
The UK release will play on at least 70 screens, with a home entertainment release also planned

articles.title: Simpsons Writer Dan Greaney Who Predicted Trump Now Runs for President
articles.summary: Dan Greaney, the Simpsons writer who famously predicted a Donald Trump presidency back in 2000, has announced his own 2028 run for the White House. Greaney is running as a progressive Republican, citing frustration with both major parties. He filed his official candidacy with the Federal Election Commission back in April, so this is very much real.
articles.content:
Back in 2000, Dan Greaney slipped a joke into a Simpsons episode called "Bart to the Future." In it, Lisa Simpson has grown up to become President of the United States and is dealing with, in her words, "quite a budget crunch from President Trump." It got a laugh. Then, 16 years later, it came true. Now Greaney has decided that if he can predict a Trump presidency, he can probably handle being president himself.

Greaney appeared on Instagram dressed in a robe, gray beard and wig to announce that "Judgment Day is here" as he launched his presidential bid. It is a very on-brand entrance for a man who has spent decades writing jokes for a living. But underneath the costume, the message is pretty serious. He then ripped the costume off, revealing a suit underneath, and announced himself as a progressive Republican candidate.

Greaney is running for 2028 as a "progressive Republican in the tradition of Abraham Lincoln and Teddy Roosevelt." His policies include universal healthcare and a Green New Deal, and an expansion of the Supreme Court from nine to 13 justices. He also wants to reduce the influence of money in politics through transparency legislation and a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission. The 2010 Supreme Court decision, for context, ruled that corporations and unions have a First Amendment right to unlimited independent political spending.

"When I saw that photo of Trump surrounded by tech billionaires and CEOs at the inauguration, I was disgusted into action. I realized that nobody was going to save us, and that self-respect required me to at least try to do something," Greaney said. Greaney is a graduate of Harvard University and its law school, and is a longtime comedy writer best known for his work on The Simpsons, The Office, and Borat. According to FEC data, Greaney filed his candidacy on April 19. Whether this turns into a serious run or stays a gloriously weird cultural moment, one thing is clear: the prophet has entered the race.
keyPoints: Dan Greaney wrote the 2000 Simpsons episode that predicted a Trump presidency
He announced his 2028 presidential run on Instagram dressed as a prophet
He is running as a progressive Republican despite backing policies like universal healthcare and a Green New Deal
Greaney filed his candidacy officially with the FEC on April 19 2026
He holds both undergraduate and law degrees from Harvard University

articles.title: Naomi Osaka Stuns Roland Garros With Germanier Upcycled Nike Reveal
articles.summary: Naomi Osaka turned her first-round French Open match on May 26 into a full fashion moment, arriving on Court Suzanne Lenglen in a custom Germanier black corset and pleated maxi skirt before peeling it back to reveal a gold sequin-adorned dress made from upcycled Nike garments underneath. The look was created by stylist Marty Harper in collaboration with Swiss designer Kevin Germanier. Osaka then went on to defeat Laura Siegemund of Germany and advance to the second round.
articles.content:
Naomi Osaka has always treated the tennis court like a runway, but at the 2026 French Open she somehow took it to another level entirely. On May 26, day three of the tournament at Stade Roland-Garros in Paris, Osaka walked out for her first-round match and stopped the crowd before she even picked up a racket.

Stylist Marty Harper collaborated with Swiss designer Germanier on an "upcycled look" for Osaka. She initially graced the Stade Roland-Garros court in a sleeveless, all-black corset, plus a matching pleated maxi skirt. Cascading crystals and sequins added an iridescent shine to the shadowy backdrop. That was just the opening act.

Her base layer turned out to be a taupe tennis dress stitched from upcycled Nike garments. She stayed true to her maximalist self by flaunting gold sequin strands atop a Nike-branded bodysuit. The whole reveal unfolded in stages, right there on court, as Osaka stripped off the Germanier layers one by one before the match began. Osaka had been asked about her outfit design in French Open press conferences but divulged nothing except to say that as a quiet person, she lets her clothing designs, colours, and patterns talk for her. They certainly did.

To finish the look, the Nike brand ambassador stayed true to tradition with her GP Challenge 1 "Naomi Osaka" sneakers in an unreleased coral tint. On day three of the two-week tournament, Osaka played her first-round match against Laura Siegemund of Germany. Hours before she defeated Siegemund, Osaka secured her first win in the fashion department.

Osaka blessed fans with not one but two trophy-worthy outfit reveals this month. Her first sartorial grand slam came at the 2026 Met Gala, where the tennis legend shed a white Robert Wun coat for a blood-red mermaid dress. This French Open look is the natural sequel to that moment. Osaka advanced to the second-round match on May 28 against Croatia's Donna Vekić. At this point, fans are equally excited for the next match and the next outfit.
keyPoints: Naomi Osaka wore a custom Germanier black corset and skirt to walk onto court at Roland Garros on May 26
She revealed a dress made from upcycled Nike garments underneath, decorated with gold sequins
The look was created by stylist Marty Harper with Swiss designer Kevin Germanier
Osaka defeated Laura Siegemund of Germany and advanced to face Donna Vekić on May 28
She also wore a showstopping Robert Wun reveal look at the 2026 Met Gala earlier in May

articles.title: AI Tarpits: How Creators Are Poisoning LLMs That Steal Their Work
articles.summary: Content creators and IP holders are fighting back against AI companies that scrape their work without permission, using tools called 'tarpits' to corrupt the data that large language models learn from. The goal is to degrade the quality of chatbot outputs so badly that users lose trust and walk away. It is a messy, creative, and increasingly urgent battle over who owns the internet's content.
articles.content:
Here is the situation. AI chatbots need to keep consuming data to get smarter. That data comes from the web. And most of the time, the AI companies doing the scraping never asked anyone for permission.

So now, some of those content owners are hitting back. They are using tools called 'tarpits' to poison the data before AI bots can eat it. The idea is simple: feed the crawler garbage, and eventually the model it trains spits out garbage too. As one explanation put it, the aim is to corrupt a chatbot's underlying large language model so that it gives 'incorrect, misleading, or utterly bonkers outputs.'

For images, tools like Nightshade have been around for a while. Nightshade works by subtly altering image pixels in ways humans cannot see but that completely confuse AI training systems. The problem is that most chatbots run on text, not pictures. So a wave of newer text-focused tarpits has emerged to fill the gap. These tools target the web crawlers that AI companies send out to harvest blog posts, articles, and other written content. Instead of blocking the bots, tarpits lure them in and feed them endless streams of plausible-sounding nonsense, wasting their compute and slipping corrupted data into training pipelines.

The stakes are real. Research has shown that poisoning a model does not take as much effort as you might think. Studies have found that even a tiny fraction of corrupted data can measurably degrade a model's outputs. The broader question is whether tarpits will actually change AI companies' behavior, or whether this turns into an arms race where both sides just keep building better tools. Either way, creators are done waiting for lawsuits to catch up. They are taking matters into their own hands right now.
keyPoints: AI companies routinely scrape websites to train their models without asking content owners for consent
Tarpits are tools that lure AI crawlers in and feed them corrupted or meaningless data to poison the model
Image poisoning tools like Nightshade exist but do not work against text-focused AI systems
Newer text-based tarpits are now emerging to target the web crawlers that harvest written content
Even a small amount of poisoned data can noticeably degrade what a chatbot produces
articles.wikiSearchQuery: AI data poisoning large language models

articles.title: Chess Closes June 21 as Lea Michele Exit Kills the Show
articles.summary: Broadway's Chess revival will close on June 21, the same day Lea Michele takes her final bow, ending the run three months earlier than planned. The show had already announced Joanna "JoJo" Levesque as Michele's replacement, but producers pulled the plug before JoJo could perform a single show. Declining ticket sales and a bruising Tony Awards snub appear to have sealed the musical's fate.
articles.content:
Broadway's Chess revival is calling it a game. The show will close on June 21 at the Imperial Theatre, producers Tom Hulce, Robert Ahrens, and The Shubert Organization confirmed Tuesday. That date was already marked on the calendar as Lea Michele's final performance, and now it will also be the last night the show exists at all.

Although Lea Michele was scheduled to depart the production in late June, the musical had intended to remain on Broadway with Joanna "JoJo" Levesque set to take over the lead role of Florence Vassy through at least September. JoJo appears to have been blindsided by the news. JoJo's casting was announced back in March and she was hyping up her Broadway return just one day ago on Instagram, sharing footage from playing the Forever Mine Festival in Chicago, which she said was her "last show before I go into Chess." She never got the chance.

A quick bit of backstory: Chess is a musical with a notoriously troubled history. It began as a concept album from ABBA members Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus and Evita's Tim Rice before the show opened on London's West End in 1986. A very different version premiered in the U.S. in 1988 and flopped, closing after two months. This 2026 revival was its first return to Broadway and featured a new book by Emmy Award winner Danny Strong of Dopesick fame, directed by Michael Mayer.

So what went wrong this time? The money tells the story. The production hit its highest gross of $2 million Thanksgiving week, but in recent weeks has been bringing in under $900,000 with capacity around 70 percent. The most dramatic drop came during Michele's April vacation, when the Imperial Theatre's occupancy fell to 66%. That is a painful indicator of just how much the show depended on one star.

The musical had a weaker-than-expected showing when Tony Award nominations were announced in early May. Although Chess scored five nods, including lead actor for Nicholas Christopher, featured actor for Bryce Pinkham, and featured actress for Hannah Cruz, the show's two biggest stars, Michele and Aaron Tveit, were snubbed, while the production was not recognized in the musical revival category. Missing the Best Revival of a Musical nomination was effectively a death sentence for a show already struggling at the box office.

Producers have not disclosed a capitalization figure for the production, though some media reports put the figure at around $15 million, and they will not recoup the investment. The cast recording of the revival is already out digitally, with a CD and vinyl release still planned for later this year. If you want to catch Chess live, June 21 is your absolute last chance.
keyPoints: Chess will close on Broadway on June 21, three months ahead of schedule
The closing date matches Lea Michele's planned exit from the show
JoJo Levesque was set to replace Michele starting June 23 but will never perform in the show
Weekly grosses had fallen from a $2 million peak to under $900,000 with only 70% capacity
Chess received five Tony nominations but was snubbed in the Best Revival of a Musical category

articles.title: Loewe Dresses Spain's Football Teams Through 2030 World Cup
articles.summary: Loewe has signed a four-year deal to outfit Spain's national men's and women's football teams off the pitch, starting at the 2026 FIFA World Cup and running through the 2030 tournament. The collection covers tailored suiting, casualwear, footwear, and leather goods, all shaped by creative directors Jack McCollough and Lázaro Hernández. It is a major moment for a luxury house that is rooting its global expansion in its Spanish identity right as the world's biggest sporting event kicks off on home-adjacent turf.
articles.content:
Loewe just made the most stylish play of the 2026 World Cup season, and the ball has not even been kicked yet. Loewe has signed a four-year partnership with Spain's national football teams, outfitting both the men's and women's sides from the 2026 FIFA World Cup all the way through to the 2030 tournament, which Spain, Portugal, and Morocco will co-host.

As part of the alliance, Loewe will design the complete travel wardrobe for both national sides across all international tournaments. The collection, conceived under the creative direction of Jack McCollough and Lázaro Hernández, includes casualwear, tailoring, footwear, and leather goods, blending the house's artisanal style with a sporting spirit. This is not a generic sports sponsorship. Loewe is treating the Spanish squad like they are walking a runway, not boarding a team bus.

Standout pieces include a suit developed in the Loewe workshops, with the Loewe Anagram subtly embroidered on the inside of the sleeve. That kind of quiet detail is very Loewe. The house has always been more about craft than flash, and that sensibility fits surprisingly well with the understated confidence of a Spain team that has been winning everything in sight.

Founded in Madrid in 1846, Loewe is part of the LVMH group's portfolio, which also includes brands such as Dior, Louis Vuitton, Givenchy, and Bulgari. Having a Spanish luxury house dress the Spanish national team for a World Cup that opens on June 11 feels almost too perfect. Loewe gets a global stage right as its cultural relevance is at an all-time high, and Spain's players get to look like the most well-dressed squad in North America.

The timing also matters because of where football and fashion are right now. Luxury houses have been circling sport for years, but deals like this one, covering both men's and women's teams equally over four years, signal something more serious than a one-off campaign. Loewe is planting a flag in football culture for the long haul.
keyPoints: Loewe signed a four-year deal to dress Spain's men's and women's national football teams
The partnership covers all major tournaments from the 2026 World Cup through 2030
Creative directors Jack McCollough and Lázaro Hernández shaped the collection
The range includes tailoring, casualwear, footwear, and leather goods
Loewe was founded in Madrid in 1846 and is owned by LVMH

articles.title: Thom Yorke Joins Flea at KOKO Playing 'Traffic Lights' and Marvin Gaye
articles.summary: Thom Yorke surprised the crowd at KOKO London by jumping on stage with Flea for a two-song set that felt like a genuine gift. The former Atoms For Peace bandmates played their recent collaboration 'Traffic Lights' and then covered Marvin Gaye's classic 'Got To Give It Up'. It was the kind of spontaneous live moment that reminds you why nothing beats being in the room.
articles.content:
If you were at KOKO in London last night, you got one of those stories you will be telling for years. Thom Yorke, the Radiohead frontman, walked out on stage during Flea's show and the place absolutely lost its mind.

The two played their recent collaboration 'Traffic Lights' first, giving the song its live debut in front of a real crowd. If you haven't heard it yet, 'Traffic Lights' is the track that reunited Yorke and Flea after their time together in Atoms For Peace, the supergroup that also featured producer Nigel Godrich and drummer Joey Waronker. That band last released music over a decade ago, so any new joint work from these two is a big deal for fans.

Then they went full party mode. Yorke and Flea closed out the surprise set with a cover of Marvin Gaye's 1977 funk classic 'Got To Give It Up', which is exactly the kind of wildly joyful song choice you would not necessarily predict from Thom Yorke but makes total sense once you hear it. The crowd clearly went with it.

Moments like this are what make small venue shows so special. KOKO holds around 1,500 people, which means everyone there was close enough to feel it properly. No stadium production, no pyrotechnics. Just two legends on a stage having fun together. The clip is already circulating online if you missed it.
keyPoints: Thom Yorke made a surprise appearance at Flea's KOKO London show on 27 May 2026
The pair performed their recent collaboration 'Traffic Lights' live
They also covered Marvin Gaye's 'Got To Give It Up'
Yorke and Flea were previously bandmates in the supergroup Atoms For Peace
Video of the performance is circulating online

articles.title: Tooleko Wins Cooper's Hill Cheese Roll for Third Year Running
articles.summary: German YouTuber Tom Kopke, known online as Tooleko, has claimed his third consecutive win at the Cooper's Hill Cheese-Rolling and Wake in Gloucestershire, England. This year he beat Guinness World Record holder Chris Anderson, who had come out of retirement just to face him. Kopke, 24, said his goal was to "cement my name in greatness in cheese rolling glory."
articles.content:
Some people chase fame. Tom Kopke chases cheese. The 24-year-old German YouTuber, known online as Tooleko, has just won the Cooper's Hill Cheese-Rolling and Wake in Brockworth, Gloucestershire for the third year in a row, completing a hat-trick that has made him one of the most unlikely sports legends on the internet.

In case you have never heard of it, Cooper's Hill Cheese-Rolling is exactly what it sounds like. Thousands of people gather to watch competitors from all over the world chase a 7lb wheel of Double Gloucester cheese, which can reach speeds of up to 70mph, down a 1:2 gradient slope. The tradition dates back nearly two centuries as a local custom in the Cotswolds area of the UK. It is chaotic, painful, and absolutely brilliant.

This year's race had an extra storyline. Tom beat Brockworth competitor and Guinness World Record holder Chris Anderson, known as "King of the Hill" after winning the event 23 times. Anderson had returned from retirement specifically to face Kopke's challenge, adding real intensity to this year's contest. He did not take the win, but he was awarded his Guinness World Record prize. Conditions made the battle even tougher. Heavy spring rain had softened the hill, but recent hot weather dried it out and made conditions firmer and more dangerous for competitors. Temperatures hit a glaring 30 degrees in Gloucestershire on race day.

Kopke was not complaining though. He travelled from Munich to retain his title and said the conditions were "optimal" this year. He said he wanted to "cement my name in greatness for a cheese rolling glory," adding that "you can never be frightened if you want to go down, so you just shut off your brain and then go for it," and that the entire race he could see Chris Anderson in front of him and kept pushing to catch him. Tooleko's wins in 2024 and 2025 had already built him a loyal online following, but his success marks another international win in a competition long dominated by British entrants but increasingly popular worldwide. Three in a row cements him as the defining face of the world's most wonderfully unhinged sporting event.
keyPoints: Tom Kopke, known online as Tooleko, won the Cooper's Hill Cheese-Rolling and Wake for the third consecutive year in 2026
He beat Guinness World Record holder Chris Anderson, nicknamed King of the Hill, who came out of retirement for the race
The 7lb Double Gloucester cheese can reach speeds of up to 70mph on the steep 1:2 gradient slope
Race day temperatures hit 30 degrees Celsius, making the already firm and dangerous hill even more challenging
Kopke travels from Munich each year to compete, and now holds the record for the most consecutive wins in recent history

articles.title: Doritos Is Giving Away $250K to Build Chip Landmarks
articles.summary: Doritos has launched a contest challenging fans to build replicas of famous American landmarks entirely out of chips, with a $250,000 prize on the line. The contest is tied to America's 250th anniversary celebration in 2026. It is exactly the kind of unhinged, snack-fuelled fun that only Doritos could pull off.
articles.content:
America is turning 250 this year, and Doritos has decided the best way to celebrate is to get people stacking chips into the shape of the Statue of Liberty. Or the Grand Canyon. Or whatever landmark your Dorito-dusted hands can manage.

Doritos has launched an official contest asking fans to build replicas of famous American landmarks out of Doritos chips. The winner walks away with $250,000. That is a genuinely life-changing amount of money for what is, at its core, a very messy arts and crafts project.

The timing makes sense. America's 250th anniversary is a huge cultural moment in 2026, and brands across the country are racing to attach themselves to it. Doritos is doing it in the most Doritos way possible: loud, orange-dusted, and completely unserious. It is a smart move. Contests like this generate enormous amounts of user-created content, and watching people attempt to sculpt the Lincoln Memorial out of triangular corn chips is the kind of thing the internet was literally built for.

If you have ever wanted a legitimate reason to buy several industrial-sized bags of Doritos, this is it. The $250,000 prize means the stakes are real, even if the medium is gloriously absurd. Keep an eye on social media over the coming weeks because the builds people come up with are going to be something else entirely.
keyPoints: Doritos is running a contest where fans build famous American landmarks out of chips
The prize is $250,000
The contest celebrates America's 250th anniversary in 2026
The campaign is designed to generate viral user-created content
